2604.11131.title: MADQRL: Distributed Quantum Reinforcement Learning Framework for Multi-Agent Environments
2604.11131.paper_summary: Reinforcement learning (RL) is one of the most practical ways to learn from real-life use-cases. Motivated from the cognitive methods used by humans makes it a widely acceptable strategy in the field of artificial intelligence. Most of the environments used for RL are often high-dimensional, and traditional RL algorithms becomes computationally expensive and challenging to effectively learn from such systems. Recent advancements in practical demonstration of quantum computing (QC) theories, such as compact encoding, enhanced representation and learning algorithms, random sampling, or the inherent stochastic nature of quantum systems, have opened up new directions to tackle these challenges. Quantum reinforcement learning (QRL) is seeking significant traction over the past few years. However, the current state of quantum hardware is not enough to cater for such high-dimensional environments with complex multi-agent setup. To tackle this issue, we propose a distributed framework for QRL where multiple agents learn independently, distributing the load of joint training from individual machines. Our method works well for environments with disjoint sets of action and observation spaces, but can also be extended to other systems with reasonable approximations. We analyze the proposed method on cooperative-pong environment and our results indicate ~10% improvement from other distribution strategies, and ~5% improvement from classical models of policy representation.
2604.11131.paper_summary_zh: 強化學習（RL）是從現實生活案例中學習的最實用方法之一。受人類使用的認知方法啟發，使其在人工智慧領域成為一種廣泛接受的策略。大多數用於強化學習的環境通常是高維的，傳統的強化學習算法在計算上變得昂貴，並且在這些系統中有效學習具有挑戰性。最近在量子計算（QC）理論的實際應用方面的進展，例如緊湊編碼、增強表示和學習算法、隨機抽樣或量子系統固有的隨機性，為解決這些挑戰開辟了新的方向。量子強化學習（QRL）在過去幾年中獲得了顯著的關注。然而，目前的量子硬體尚不足以應對如此高維的環境和複雜的多代理設置。為了解決這個問題，我們提出了一個分佈式的量子強化學習框架，其中多個代理獨立學習，分擔來自個別機器的聯合訓練負擔。我們的方法在具有不相交的行動和觀察空間的環境中運作良好，但也可以擴展到其他系統，並進行合理的近似。我們在合作乒乓環境中分析了所提出的方法，我們的結果顯示與其他分佈策略相比，提高了約10%，與經典政策表示模型相比，提高了約5%。
2604.11131.author: Abhishek Sawaika et.al.
2604.11131.authors: Abhishek Sawaika, Samuel Yen-Chi Chen, Udaya Parampalli, Rajkumar Buyya
